title: Navigation History, Back Semantics, Snapshots, and Failure Recovery · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed ranger review reconstructs navigation history, back semantics, snapshots, and failure recovery from revision, command, arguments, request identity, response fixture, state transition, cache decision, goroutine ownership, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded snapshot navigation history after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Define whether back means prior successful view, API previous link, or numeric page and expose that contract in help.
Store immutable-enough page snapshots or identifiers with explicit copy boundaries so later mutation cannot rewrite history.
Push history only after a new view is admitted and leave cursor plus history unchanged on failure or cancellation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review Go team handles case 3872 during a narrow-terminal ranger workflow. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate bounded successful-view snapshots and failure-safe back behavior; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
